_convention: Fields prefixed with _ are system-managed (auto_generated, source, schema_version, sourceFile, generatedAt, meta). Authored content uses bare keys; system metadata uses _-prefix. Reserved for future extensions.
_naming_convention: Every key in paths and collections is leaf XOR namespace, never both. A key cannot resolve to a file AND be a prefix of nested children. To add a sibling artifact under an existing leaf, rename the leaf to a sub-key first (e.g., accessibility.guide + accessibility.index, not accessibility + accessibility.index). Enforced by tests/manifest-convention.test.js.

accessibility.authoring.description: How to edit the accessibility per-section files (UX team guide)

accessibility.bundle.description: Full nested accessibility tree (hierarchical Pattern H roll-up, mirrors foundations.bundle). One-shot LLM consumption: every section/leaf reachable from this single file.

accessibility.index.description: Slug → WCAG mapping for accessibility sections; consumed by Phase 2 category defaults + future MCP server.

accessibility.sections.description: Per-section block-JSON for accessibility (mirrors foundations dist); slug-shared with accessibility.index. Root metadata + top-level child list; navigate from here to per-section directories. Sibling files under accessibility/dist/.

appContext.description: Per-app patterns + persona + terminology (consolidated dist — do not edit)

appContextBundle.description: Self-contained bundle wrapping the app-context dist under an `appContext` key; consumed by tools that embed the whole domain in a single JSON.

components.anatomy.bundle.description: Roll-up of every per-component anatomy JSON, keyed by slug. One-shot LLM consumption for structure-tree access across the whole DS.

components.canonicalSections.description: Canonical design-guideline section list (heading, stable anchor, aliases, media role) — single source for the editor stub scaffolder and the docs design-section renderer.

components.categories.description: DS Kit component category groupings (Action, Form (input & selection), Navigation, Data Display, Feedback, Overlays) derived from the Figma Pages panel naming convention. Generated nightly by the Figma sync.

components.categoryDefaults.action.description: Per-category structural defaults (anatomy, variants, motion refs, a11y refs) for the 'action' category. Consumed by the component-brief skill for stub components.

components.categoryDefaults.bundle.description: Roll-up of all 6 category-defaults JSONs keyed by slug. One-shot LLM consumption for category-aware brief enrichment.

components.categoryDefaults.data-display.description: Per-category structural defaults (anatomy, variants, motion refs, a11y refs) for the 'data-display' category. Consumed by the component-brief skill for stub components.

components.categoryDefaults.feedback.description: Per-category structural defaults (anatomy, variants, motion refs, a11y refs) for the 'feedback' category. Consumed by the component-brief skill for stub components.

components.categoryDefaults.form-input-s.description: Per-category structural defaults (anatomy, variants, motion refs, a11y refs) for the 'form-input-selection' category. Consumed by the component-brief skill for stub components.

components.categoryDefaults.navigation.description: Per-category structural defaults (anatomy, variants, motion refs, a11y refs) for the 'navigation' category. Consumed by the component-brief skill for stub components.

components.categoryDefaults.overlays.description: Per-category structural defaults (anatomy, variants, motion refs, a11y refs) for the 'overlays' category. Consumed by the component-brief skill for stub components.

components.categoryOverrides.description: Curated slug -> category display-name overrides consumed by scripts/graph/derive-graph.js when a registry component carries no category (Figma frames on a category-header page). Registry wins when present; entries self-retire after the Figma page reorg + re-sync. Provenance manual-override.

components.categoryPageOverrides.description: Page clean-name -> canonical category overrides plus a staging-exclude list, consumed by scripts/transformers/transform-categories.js during the Figma sync when a page's own name does not encode its category (a WIP-renamed or self-hosting icons page). Applied first in inferCategoryMap; self-retiring after the Figma pages are reorganized and a re-sync restores the category. Provenance manual-override.

components.iconGroups.description: Icon → semantic group mapping (ζ.5). Curated from the Figma Actual-icons layout; consumed by sync orchestrator to populate registry entries' `group` + `secondaryGroups` fields for items in category === 'Icons'. Key order = priority for primary-group resolution (specific-first).

components.icons.src.description: Curated icon SVG geometry ({viewBox, body} per slug) for the hi-fi render tier. Hand-extracted from Figma (REST format=svg -> normalize fills to currentColor). Provenance (dsKey/nodeId/group) is joined at derive time from the registry + icon-groups.json. Source for components.icons.svg.

components.icons.svg.description: Derived icon-geometry read-surface: per-slug {viewBox, body, group, dsKey, nodeId}. Consumed by the plugin render tier's renderIcon() to inline real glyphs. Auto-exported to the monochrome UI-icon set (~145 = Icons category minus the Connector group) by the icons sync phase, layered under the curated overrides; multicolor connector logos are a separate future asset class.

components.media.index.description: Slug → role-keyed media map for every component with at least one CI-generated asset under components/dist/media/<slug>/. Decoupled from guideline coverage: components without authored or pattern-synthesized guidelines still surface their media here. Consumers (docs MediaAsset, plugin component-brief, future MCP) read this to enumerate media availability without filesystem scans.

components.registries.dskit.description: DS Kit component registry (Figma REST sync)

components.registries.fmkit.description: FM (wireframe) Kit component registry (Figma REST sync)

components.registries.metakit.description: Meta Kit component registry — templates block hand-curated, preserved across syncs

components.registries.styles.description: DS Kit text + effect styles (Figma REST sync)

content.globalMd.description: Global / cross-cutting content guidelines only (voice, tone, capitalization, words to avoid, UX-pattern topics, Actian product-surface rules) — auto-generated from content/src/{writing,patterns,product}/*.md plus root-level meta files. The docs /content page and the markdown-consuming plugin skills read this; component-scoped content guidance lives per-component in components/dist/guidelines/{slug}.json instead.

content.patternsMd.description: The patterns bucket of the global content guidelines, split out (UX-pattern content topics: empty/system states, forms, notifications, onboarding, validation, wizards). Same sections as global.md's patterns family, same index order. Note the same source sections also fan out per-component into components/dist/guidelines/{slug}.json.

content.productMd.description: The product bucket of the global content guidelines, split out (Actian product-surface rules: lineage UI, object preview panels, related-content panels). Same sections as global.md's product family, same index order.

content.wordsToAvoid.description: Structured words-to-avoid rules (content's first JSON dist), parsed from content/src/writing/words-to-avoid.md. Each rule = { avoid: [tokens], reason, example: { do, dont } }; advisory rules carry avoid: []. Consumers read avoid[] for programmatic checks; global.md keeps the prose table for humans.

content.writingMd.description: The writing bucket of the global content guidelines, split out (voice, tone, capitalization, punctuation, words to avoid with the rendered Do/Don't table). Same sections as global.md's writing family, same index order; a consumer that needs only writing rules reads this instead of the full concat. Root-level ground rules (global-guidelines) stay in global.md only.

foundations.authoring.description: How to edit the foundations per-section files (UX team guide)

foundations.bundle.description: Full nested foundations tree (hierarchical Pattern H roll-up). One-shot LLM consumption: every section/leaf reachable from this single file.

foundations.color-primitives.description: Hierarchical _index for foundations section 'Color Primitives'. Children listed inside; sibling files under foundations/dist/color-primitives/.

foundations.design-guidelines.description: Hierarchical _index for foundations section 'Design Guidelines'. Children listed inside; sibling files under foundations/dist/design-guidelines/.

foundations.index.description: Root foundations metadata + top-level child list. Navigate from here to per-H2 directories.

foundations.source.description:
Synthesized concatenation of foundations/src/ per-section files joined with `

---

` (Stripe .md URL pattern). Auto-synced; do not edit. The substrate-side SoT is the per-section files; this dist artifact bakes section separators that don't exist in any single source file.

foundations.tokens.index.description: Hierarchical _index for foundations section 'Tokens'. Children listed inside; sibling files under foundations/dist/tokens/.

foundations.tokens.motion.description: Structured motion leaf (tokens + patterns) for foundations section 'Tokens'. Resolved directly by motion-ref lookup in the plugin + docs.

graph.bundle.description: Derived knowledge graph: typed nodes (component/category/a11y_criterion/foundation_section/motion_pattern/content_topic/app/app_entity/terminology_term/ux_pattern) + typed edges (in_category, a11y_ref, motion_ref, foundations_ref, related, narrower, in_app, entity_related, term_about). Transversal refs are category-mediated; app-context adds a product-domain island (apps, entities, terminology, UX patterns) linked by in_app / entity_related / term_about. Component nodes additionally carry figmaKey / figmaNodeId (stable Figma identifiers) as queryable external ids; the slug stays the node id. Consumed by future graph viz + AI agents.

graph.collisions.description: Cross-registry component slug collisions (slug present in >1 kit with distinct Figma keys), each with the candidate {kit, key, nodeId} and the kit the graph node resolved to. Makes the silent first-wins tie-break legible.

graph.context.description: Hand-authored JSON-LD @context for the knowledge graph: maps node-id prefixes, node/edge type terms, and field terms to IRIs (reusing schema.org/SKOS/PROV; actian-ds: for DS-specific concepts). Governs graph/dist/graph.jsonld. Keep in sync with graph/vocabulary.json.

graph.jsonld.description: Derived linked-data (JSON-LD) view of the knowledge graph: the same nodes + edges as graph.bundle, wrapped with graph/context.jsonld, node @types, and reified edges. Structurally lossless (every node and edge field is carried through). Additive sibling of graph/dist/graph.json (which stays canonical). A JSON-LD 1.1 document ready for a linked-data reader; full RDF/SPARQL round-tripping (a stable IRI base plus source-file provenance as dereferenceable IRIs) is deferred until the IRI base domain is chosen (spec open decision).

graph.quality-report.description: Derived graph quality/health report: per-dimension metrics — coverage (authored transversal refs that became edges), integrity violation counts, connectivity/orphan counts — each tagged with a severity tier (violation/warning/info). Emitted by validate-graph.js; timestamp injected by CI at publish. Trendable graph-health signal.

tokens.css.description: CSS custom properties (--zen-*) — now generator-owned (P4b flip). Emitted by derive-tokens.js via emit-css.js from the live tokens.json tree.

tokens.derivedNumerics.description: Generated numeric/dimension token layer (DTCG: spacing, border, focus-ring, font, icon, size, shadow, motion + Figma bindings) — staging output of derive-tokens.js (P3)

tokens.derivedPrimitives.description: Generated primitive color layer (DTCG: hex + oklch + Figma binding) — staging output of derive-tokens.js (P1)

tokens.derivedSemantics.description: Generated semantic color layer (DTCG: alias $value + 3-theme hex expansion + Figma binding) — staging output of derive-tokens.js (P2)

tokens.figmaBindingsRaw.description: Figma variable + style binding harvest (MCP search export) — input to the tokens deriver binding sidecar (P0)

tokens.figmaVariableIds.description: Figma local-variable id -> stable library key map (exported by scripts/figma-plugin, manual run) — the P2 name-layer join input; empty ids = values-only capture

tokens.json.description: DTCG W3C-format design tokens — now generator-owned (P4b flip). Written by derive-tokens.js from color-primitives.md + tokens.md + figma-bindings-raw.json. _frozen:false.

accessibility.guide.description: WCAG 2.2 AA guidance, authored as per-section files (intro, principles, … designer-handoff-checklist). Canonical order is declared in `_order.json` (sibling manifest); consumers either follow that order or fall back to alphabetical. AUTHORING.md is excluded.

accessibility.leaf.description: Per-leaf accessibility section JSONs in hierarchical Pattern H layout (mirrors foundations.leaf). Each leaf mirrors its MD heading path. Branch nodes carry an `_index.json` instead. Single roll-up at accessibility.bundle. Recursive: any file under accessibility/dist/ is covered by this collection.

appContextSrc.description: Authoring surface for app context. Per-kind subdirs: apps, entities, patterns. Terminology lives in terminology.yml (not covered by this collection).

components.anatomy.byKey.description: Per-component anatomy JSONs (normalized structure tree, Figma-synced). See components.anatomy.bundle for the roll-up.

components.categoriesSrc.description: Authoring surface for category defaults. YAML frontmatter governed by schemas/category-defaults.json; freeform body for design rationale. See AUTHORING.md.

components.categoryDefaults.byKey.description: Per-category defaults (Phase 2 v2). 6 categories: action, form-input-selection, navigation, data-display, feedback, overlays. See also bundle entry components.categoryDefaults.bundle for the roll-up. Renamed from components.categoryDefaults in v0.5.1 to honor leaf-XOR-namespace manifest convention.

components.guidelineDoc.byKey.description: Per-component merged guideline objects. See components.guidelineDoc.bundle for the roll-up.

components.guidelineDocSrc.description: Authoring surface for per-component guidelines. Each {slug}/ directory holds _meta.yml + optional per-domain files (content.md, usage.md, design.md, behavior.md, tokens.yml). Recursive: covers everything under components/src/ — the per-component {slug}/ trees plus the sibling categories/ and guidelines/ source dirs (which also carry their own collections). See components/src/AUTHORING.md.

components.icons.dist.description: The icons dist directory. Holds the derived read-surface icons.json (documented explicitly as components.icons.svg) plus the auto-generated icons.degraded.json worklist: the slugs the icons sync phase could not normalize (reasons: bad-viewbox, multicolor, gradient-or-image-fill, render-failed, node-missing, empty), mirroring anatomy's quality.degraded honesty. `node-missing` is not a drawing problem. It means the registry advertises a component whose Figma node no longer exists (verified against /v1/files/:key/nodes), so the registry itself is stale. A collection (not a per-file path) so the degraded worklist is covered without requiring the file to pre-exist on a clean checkout.

components.images.description: Per-component static visual assets (anatomy callouts, hand-made diagrams). Optional per component — authors drop PNGs/SVGs into components/src/<slug>/images/ alongside their MD files. Consumed by docs site + plugin via manifest indirection.

components.media.ci.description: Per-component CI-generated visual assets (Figma frame captures, state matrices, motion clips). Authored by the media-preview sync phase (and future media-* phases). Single-capture roles (capture:"first", e.g. `preview`) produce `<role>.webp`; multi-capture roles (capture:"all", e.g. `parts`, `variations`, `spacing`, `behavior`, `layout`) produce `<role>-0.webp`, `<role>-1.webp`, … ordered by Figma frame index. Frames are rendered by Figma as PNG and transcoded to WebP locally (the Figma REST API has no WebP format). Vendor pull copies the tree; consumers read paths from the media index (components/dist/media/_index.json).

content.section.description: Global / cross-cutting content guidelines (Content lead). Three sub-buckets: writing/ (strict grammar/voice/style rules), patterns/ (universal UX patterns), product/ (Actian product-surface copy rules). Meta files (global-guidelines.md, content-index.md, format-spec.md) sit at the content/src/ root. Each file follows When-to-use -> Style -> Behavior -> Do/Don't.

foundations.guide.description: Master reference for Actian DS — primitives, tokens, scales, design rules. Authored as per-section files (intro, table-of-contents, color-primitives, tokens, design-guidelines, handoff-protocol, related-guidelines). Canonical order is declared in `_order.json` (sibling manifest); consumers either follow that order or fall back to alphabetical. AUTHORING.md is excluded.

foundations.leaf.description: Per-leaf foundations JSONs in hierarchical Pattern H layout. Each leaf mirrors its MD heading path. Branch nodes carry an `_index.json` instead. Single roll-up at foundations.bundle. Recursive: any file under foundations/dist/ is covered by this collection.

_notes.foundations_auto: foundations.* entries are auto-regenerated by scripts/foundations/derive-foundations.js. Do not hand-edit. Hierarchical layout: bundle for one-shot, per-H2 _index entries for navigation; full per-leaf tree lives under foundations/dist/<slug>/.
_notes.categories_auto: components.categoryDefaults.* entries are auto-regenerated by scripts/categories/derive-categories.js. Do not hand-edit. Per-category dist JSONs + a single bundle + collection entries.
_notes.guideline_doc_auto: components.guidelineDoc.* entries are auto-regenerated by scripts/components/derive-guidelines.js. Do not hand-edit. Per-component merged JSONs + a bundle + a coverage report + collection entries.
_notes.registry_aliases_interim: registryAliases maps a registry component key (e.g. 'checkbox-with-label' from components/dist/registries/dskit.json) to the components.guidelineDoc.byKey slug its guideline is authored under ('checkbox'). It exists because component-guideline authoring slugs and Figma/registry component keys evolved separately. INTERIM: the long-term fix is naming convergence, not a growing map — see components/src/AUTHORING.md 'Slug naming'. Hand-maintained; scripts/components/derive-guidelines.js READS this block (each entry makes it emit a registry-alias copy of the named guideline doc) but does not WRITE it; tests/manifest.test.js validates every key, value, and emitted alias file resolves. WHY IT MATTERS: consumers resolve a guideline by REGISTRY KEY, so a guideline with no key and no alias is derived, bundled, reported 'approved' in coverage.md, and rendered by nobody. On 2026-07-14 the card and tag family docs (both authored as family-level guidance: 'This guideline covers the tag family: default, status, stage, ...') were bridged to their 10 registry members, which had been showing no guidance at all while the plugin's component-brief silently GENERATED replacement text for them. Six authored guidelines still reach no consumer (combo-box, multi-select, global-toast, inline-toast, success-state, upload-file); each is named with a reason in tests/guideline-reachability.test.js, which fails on any NEW unreachable guideline, so this class of gap can no longer land silently.
_notes.zones: _zones classifies each manifest paths/collections artifact by role for external-team legibility (see ARCHITECTURE.md): knowledge (foundations/components/content/accessibility), contract (graph + cross-cutting connector artifacts), metadata (app-context, tokens). Keyed by top-level manifest-key prefix. '_zones._pendingEviction' lists consumer-specific artifacts slated to move to the plugin (structure-audit Track E); now empty — Track E complete (fmToDsMap + presentation evicted). Enforced by scripts/validate-manifest.js (validateZones) + tests/manifest-zones.test.js.
_notes.app_context_auto: appContext.* entries are auto-regenerated by scripts/app-context/derive-app-context.js. Do not hand-edit. Single consolidated dist JSON + src collection.
